title: Valid Anagram
prompt:
Given two strings `s` and `t`, determine if `t` is an **anagram** of `s` (same letters, same counts, any order).

**Example**: `is_anagram("anagram", "nagaram")` → `True`

A good "state the tradeoff" warm-up: `Counter` comparison is O(n) time / O(k) space (k = alphabet size); sorting both strings is O(n log n) but needs no extra data structure.
hint_notes: Counter(s) == Counter(t) is the O(n) idiomatic answer; sorted(s) == sorted(t) is the O(n log n) alternative that needs no import. Different lengths are an automatic False — Counter comparison already handles that correctly. Tag: counter-vs-sort-tradeoff.
